Convert `src/logic/events.rs` (2,351 LOC, 18 public functions) into a directory module. The first vertical slice extracts **only** the notification fan-out: the ~4 functions that build notification emails and the templates they use.

Target shape:

```
src/logic/events/
  mod.rs           # pub use siblings; the remaining ~14 fns
  notifications.rs # the 4 notification fns + their templates
```

Rules for this slice:
- Only notification functions move. The booking lifecycle, payment operations, and event-query functions stay where they are. Do **not** cascade this slice into further splits — those are follow-up issues.
- `events::notifications` takes `&impl EmailGateway` directly (the seam from #71) and stops being a sibling of the rest of `events.rs` for the purpose of email access.
- `api.rs` call sites for the moved functions update to `events::notifications::*`.
- Notification tests move next to `notifications.rs` and run offline via the `EmailGateway` mock from #71.

This is the first of several vertical slices on the events module — this one carries the proof that the new seams work in the biggest module in the codebase.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `src/logic/events/` directory with `mod.rs` + `notifications.rs` (+ any remaining siblings not touched by this slice)
- [ ] Exactly the 4 notification functions move; no other code moves
- [ ] `events::notifications` accepts `&impl EmailGateway` and no longer reaches across to the rest of `events.rs` for email access
- [ ] Notification tests live next to `notifications.rs` and run fully offline via the `EmailGateway` mock
- [ ] No behavior change in production paths — same emails go to the same recipients with the same content
- [ ] `cargo test` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy --all-features` is clean

## Follow-ups (out of scope for this slice)

- Extract `events::payments` into its own module
- Extract `events::bookings` into its own module
- Extract `events::queries` into its own module

Each of those is its own vertical slice and deserves its own issue after this one lands.

[open] Issue #71: Collapse the email seam into a deep EmailGateway to improve tests and remove mock sprawl
  Author: @mseele
  Status: OPEN
  URL: https://github.com/mseele/sve-backend/issues/71

  Description:
    ## What to build
    
    Reshape the existing `EmailSender` trait in `src/email.rs` (5 methods, each a 1-line wrapper around a free function) into a deep `EmailGateway` trait with three methods:
    
    - `send_messages(from: &EmailAccount, messages: Vec<Message>) -> Result<()>`
    - `build_message(account: &EmailAccount, ...) -> Result<Message>` (move `new_message_builder` and `mailbox` inside the module)
    - `account_by_type(email_type: EmailType) -> Result<EmailAccount>`
    
    Delete the three redundant mock constructors in `src/test_utils.rs` (`noop_mock`, `mock_email_sender_capturing`, `mock_email_sender_capturing_batch`); keep one canonical mock constructor. Logic modules stop calling `crate::email::new_message_builder` and `crate::email::mailbox` as free functions — those become private to the `EmailGateway` implementation.
    
    This is a vertical slice: the trait, the real adapter, the test adapter, the call-site migration, and the test that exercises the new seam.
    
    ## Acceptance criteria
    
    - [ ] `EmailGateway` trait has at most 3 methods
    - [ ] No logic module calls `email::new_message_builder` or `email::mailbox` directly (the leak across the seam is sealed)
    - [ ] `src/test_utils.rs` has exactly 1 mock constructor (down from 4)
    - [ ] `RealEmailGateway` (or renamed equivalent) replaces `RealEmailSender`
    - [ ] All existing email tests still pass
    - [ ] `cargo test` passes
    - [ ] `cargo clippy --all-features` is clean
    
    ## Blocked by
    
    None - can start immediately

1: # Issue tracker: GitHub
2: 
3: Issues and PRDs for this repo live as GitHub issues. Use the `gh` CLI for all operations.
4: 
5: ## Conventions
6: 
7: - **Create an issue**: `gh issue create --title "..." --body "..."`. Use a heredoc for multi-line bodies.
8: - **Read an issue**: `gh issue view <number> --comments`, filtering comments by `jq` and also fetching labels.
9: - **List issues**: `gh issue list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,comments --jq '[.[] | {number, title, body, labels: [.labels[].name], comments: [.comments[].body]}]'` with appropriate `--label` and `--state` filters.
10: - **Comment on an issue**: `gh issue comment <number> --body "..."`
11: - **Apply / remove labels**: `gh issue edit <number> --add-label "..."` / `--remove-label "..."`
12: - **Close**: `gh issue close <number> --comment "..."`
13: 
14: Infer the repo from `git remote -v` — `gh` does this automatically when run inside a clone.
15: 
16: ## Pull requests as a triage surface
17: 
18: **PRs as a request surface: no.** _(Set to `yes` if this repo treats external PRs as feature requests; `/triage` reads this flag.)_
19: 
20: When set to `yes`, PRs run through the same labels and states as issues, using the `gh pr` equivalents:
21: 
22: - **Read a PR**: `gh pr view <number> --comments` and `gh pr diff <number>` for the diff.
23: - **List external PRs for triage**: `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,author,authorAssociation,comments` then keep only `authorAssociation` of `CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR`, or `NONE` (drop `OWNER`/`MEMBER`/`COLLABORATOR`).
24: - **Comment / label / close**: `gh pr comment`, `gh pr edit --add-label`/`--remove-label`, `gh pr close`.
25: 
26: GitHub shares one number space across issues and PRs, so a bare `#42` may be either — resolve with `gh pr view 42` and fall back to `gh issue view 42`.
27: 
28: ## When a skill says "publish to the issue tracker"
29: 
30: Create a GitHub issue.
31: 
32: ## When a skill says "fetch the relevant ticket"
33: 
34: Run `gh issue view <number> --comments`.

(End of file - total 34 lines)
</content>

459: pub(crate) async fn send_event_reminders(
460:     pool: &PgPool,
461:     email_sender: &impl email::EmailSender,
462: ) -> Result<usize> {
463:     // get all events where a event reminder should be send to the subscribers
464:     let events = db::get_reminder_events(pool).await?;
465: 
466:     // process each event
467:     for event in &events {
468:         // prepare for message generation
469:         let email_account = event.get_associated_email_account(email_sender).await?;
470:         let message_type: MessageType = event.event_type.into();
471:         let mut messages = Vec::new();
472: 
473:         // get the subject and body (depending on the event type)
474:         let (subject, body) = match event.event_type {
475:             EventType::Fitness => (
476:                 format!("{} Info zum Kursstart", event.subject_prefix()),
477:                 include_str!("../../templates/event_reminder_fitness.txt"),
478:             ),
479:             EventType::Events => (
480:                 format!("{} Info zum Eventstart", event.subject_prefix()),
481:                 include_str!("../../templates/event_reminder_events.txt"),
482:             ),
483:         };
484: 
485:         // iterate all enrolled event subscribers (Option should never be None)
486:         if let Some(subscribers) = &event.subscribers {
487:             for subscriber in subscribers.iter().filter(|s| s.enrolled) {
488:                 // render the body for the email...
489:                 let body = template::render_event_reminder(body, event, subscriber)?;
490: 
491:                 // ...and push the email into the messages list
492:                 messages.push(
493:                     Email::new(
494:                         message_type,
495:                         subscriber.email.clone(),
496:                         subject.clone(),
497:                         body,
498:                         None,
499:                     )
500:                     .into_message(&email_account)?,
501:                 );
502:             }
503: 
504:             // send reminder emails to all event subribers
505:             email_sender.send_messages(&email_account, messages).await?;
506: 
507:             // mark reminder has been sent to the event
508:             // (to avoid duplicate sending of reminder emails)
509:             db::mark_as_reminder_sent(pool, &event.id).await?;
510:         }
511:     }
512: 
513:     // return the count of events for which the reminder has been send
514:     Ok(events.len())
515: }

856:     pub(crate) fn into_message(self, email_account: &EmailAccount) -> Result<Message> {
857:         let message_builder = crate::email::new_message_builder(email_account)?
858:             .to(self.to.parse()?)
859:             .subject(self.subject);
860:         let message = match self.attachments {
861:             Some(attachments) => {
862:                 let mut multi_part = MultiPart::mixed().singlepart(SinglePart::plain(self.content));
863:                 for attachment in attachments {
864:                     let filename = attachment.name;
865:                     let content = STANDARD.decode(&attachment.data)?;
866:                     let content_type = ContentType::parse(&attachment.mime_type)?;
867:                     multi_part = multi_part
868:                         .singlepart(Attachment::new(filename).body(content, content_type));
869:                 }
870:                 message_builder.multipart(multi_part)
871:             }
872:             None => message_builder.singlepart(SinglePart::plain(self.content)),
873:         }?;
874: 
875:         Ok(message)
876:     }
877: }

8: /// Build the plain text body for a contact message
9: fn build_contact_body(contact_message: &ContactMessage) -> String {
10:     let email = contact_message.email.trim();
11:     let mut body = format!(
12:         "Vor- und Nachname: {}\nEmail: {}\n",
13:         contact_message.name.trim(),
14:         email
15:     );
16:     if let Some(phone) = &contact_message.phone {
17:         let phone = phone.trim();
18:         if !phone.is_empty() {
19:             body.push_str(&format!("Telefon: {}\n", phone));
20:         }
21:     }
22:     body.push_str(&format!(
23:         "\nNachricht: {}\n",
24:         contact_message.message.trim()
25:     ));
26:     body
27: }

29: pub(crate) async fn message(
30:     contact_message: ContactMessage,
31:     email_sender: &impl EmailSender,
32: ) -> Result<()> {
33:     let email_account = email_sender
34:         .get_account_by_type(contact_message.message_type.into())
35:         .await?;
36: 
37:     let body = build_contact_body(&contact_message);
38: 
39:     let message = crate::email::new_message_builder(&email_account)?
40:         .subject(format!(
41:             "[Kontakt@Web] Nachricht von {}",
42:             contact_message.name
43:         ))
44:         .to(contact_message.to.parse()?)
45:         .reply_to(contact_message.email.parse()?)
46:         .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(body))?;
47: 
48:     email_sender.send_message(&email_account, message).await?;
49: 
50:     info!("Info message has been send successfully");
51: 
52:     Ok(())
53: }

55: pub(crate) async fn emails(emails: Vec<Email>, email_sender: &impl EmailSender) -> Result<()> {
56:     let mut grouped_emails: HashMap<EmailType, Vec<Email>> = HashMap::new();
57:     for email in emails {
58:         let email_type = email.message_type.into();
59:         grouped_emails.entry(email_type).or_default().push(email);
60:     }
61:     for (email_type, emails) in grouped_emails {
62:         let from = email_sender.get_account_by_type(email_type).await?;
63:         let messages = emails
64:             .into_iter()
65:             .map(|email| email.into_message(&from))
66:             .collect::<anyhow::Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
67:         email_sender.send_messages(&from, messages).await?;
68:     }
69: 
70:     Ok(())
71: }

61: async fn send_mail(subscription: NewsSubscription, email_sender: &impl EmailSender) -> Result<()> {
62:     let primary_news_topic;
63:     let multiple_topics;
64:     if subscription.topics.len() == 1 {
65:         primary_news_topic = *subscription.topics.first().unwrap();
66:         multiple_topics = None
67:     } else {
68:         primary_news_topic = NewsTopic::General;
69:         multiple_topics = Some(
70:             subscription
71:                 .topics
72:                 .iter()
73:                 .map(|topic| topic.display_name())
74:                 .collect::<Vec<&str>>()
75:                 .join(", "),
76:         );
77:     }
78:     let subject;
79:     let topic;
80:     let kind;
81:     let regards;
82:     match primary_news_topic {
83:         NewsTopic::General => {
84:             subject = "[Infos@SVE] Bestätigung Newsletter Anmeldung";
85:             topic = "News rund um den SVE";
86:             if let Some(multiple_topics) = multiple_topics {
87:                 kind = format!(" zu folgenden Themen: {}", multiple_topics);
88:             } else {
89:                 kind = ", sobald es etwas neues gibt".into();
90:             }
91:             regards = "SV Eutingen";
92:         }
93:         NewsTopic::Events => {
94:             subject = "[Events@SVE] Bestätigung Event-News Anmeldung";
95:             topic = "unseren Events";
96:             kind = ", sobald neue Events online sind".into();
97:             regards = "Team Events@SVE";
98:         }
99:         NewsTopic::Fitness => {
100:             subject = "[Fitness@SVE] Bestätigung Fitness-News Anmeldung";
101:             topic = "unseren Fitnesskursen";
102:             kind = ", sobald neue Kurse online sind".into();
103:             regards = "Team Fitness@SVE";
104:         }
105:     };
106: 
107:     let email_account = email_sender
108:         .get_account_by_type(primary_news_topic.into())
109:         .await?;
110:     let message = crate::email::new_message_builder(&email_account)?
111:         .header(header::MIME_VERSION_1_0)
112:         .header(ContentType::TEXT_PLAIN)
113:         .to(subscription.email.parse()?)
114:         .bcc(crate::email::mailbox(&email_account)?)
115:         .subject(subject)
116:         .singlepart(SinglePart::plain(format!(
117:             "Lieber Interessent/In,
118: 
119: vielen Dank für Dein Interesse an {}.
120: 
121: Ab sofort erhältst Du automatisch eine E-Mail{}.
122: 
123: {}
124: 
125: Herzliche Grüße
126: {}",
127:             topic, kind, UNSUBSCRIBE_MESSAGE, regards
128:         )))?;
129: 
130:     email_sender.send_message(&email_account, message).await?;
131: 
132:     Ok(())
133: }

324:         .headers()

326:         .and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok())

331:

335:     };

337:     let claims = match verify_session_jwt(token, &state.session_secret) {
338:         Ok(c) => c,

340:             tracing::warn!("Session JWT verification failed: {:?}", e);
341:             return (StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, "Invalid session token").into_response();
342:         }
343:     };
344: 
345:     if !authorize(&claims, &state.allowed_emails, &state.allowed_domain) {
346:         return (StatusCode::FORBIDDEN, "Access denied").into_response();
347:     }

349:     next.run(req).await

433: async fn verify_google_token(token: &str, state: &AppState) -> Result<Claims> {
434:     let header = decode_header(token)?;
435:     let kid = header.kid.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Missing kid in JWT"))?;
436: 
437:     let decoding_key = {
438:         let needs_refresh = {
439:             let jwks_cache = state.jwks.read().await;
440:             jwks_cache.is_expired() || !jwks_cache.keys.contains_key(&kid)
441:         };
442:         if needs_refresh {
443:             tracing::info!("Refreshing JWKS cache (expired or missing kid: {})", kid);
444:             let new_keys = fetch_jwks(
445:                 &state.http_client,
446:                 "https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/certs",
447:             )
448:             .await?;
449:             let mut jwks_cache = state.jwks.write().await;
450:             jwks_cache.keys = new_keys;
451:             jwks_cache.last_updated = std::time::Instant::now();
452:         }
453:         let jwks_cache = state.jwks.read().await;
454:         jwks_cache
455:             .keys
456:             .get(&kid)
457:             .cloned()
458:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Unknown JWT key"))?
459:     };
460: 
461:     let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::RS256);
462:     validation.validate_aud = false;
463:     let token_data = decode::<Claims>(token, &decoding_key, &validation)?;
464:     Ok(token_data.claims)
465: }

467: fn mint_session_jwt(email: &str, hd: Option<&str>, secret: &str) -> Result<String> {
468:     let now = Utc::now().timestamp() as usize;
469:     let claims = Claims {
470:         email: email.to_string(),
471:         hd: hd.map(|s| s.to_string()),
472:         exp: now + 30 * 24 * 60 * 60,
473:         iat: now,
474:     };
475:     let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(
476:         &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),
477:         &claims,
478:         &EncodingKey::from_secret(secret.as_bytes()),
479:     )?;
480:     Ok(token)
481: }
482: 
483: fn verify_session_jwt(token: &str, secret: &str) -> Result<Claims> {
484:     let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);
485:     validation.validate_aud = false;
486:     validation.validate_exp = true;
487:     let token_data = decode::<Claims>(
488:         token,
489:         &DecodingKey::from_secret(secret.as_bytes()),
490:         &validation,
491:     )?;
492:     Ok(token_data.claims)
493: }

514:                 .write_str("a string containing a comma separated list of lifecycle status strings")

518:         where
519:             E: de::Error,

923: /// Validates the provided captcha token using the hCaptcha service.
924: /// Returns Ok(()) if the captcha is valid, or a ResponseError if validation fails.
925: async fn validate_captcha(
926:     token: &Option<String>,
927:     client_ip: Option<IpAddr>,
928:     secrets: &dyn SecretProvider,
929: ) -> Result<(), ResponseError> {
930:     let token = token.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| ResponseError {
931:         err: anyhow::anyhow!("No captcha token provided"),
932:         response: Some((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Captcha token is required.".into())),
933:     })?;
934: 
935:     let secret = &secrets.get(SecretKey::CaptchaSecret).await?;
936: 
937:     let captcha = hcaptcha::Captcha::new(token.as_str()).map_err(|e| ResponseError {
938:         err: anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to create captcha: {:?}", e),
939:         response: Some((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Invalid captcha token.".into())),
940:     })?;
941: 
942:     let mut request = hcaptcha::Request::new(secret, captcha).map_err(|e| ResponseError {
943:         err: anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to build captcha request: {:?}", e),
944:         response: Some((
945:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
946:             "Captcha validation failed.".into(),
947:         )),
948:     })?;
949: 
950:     if let Some(ip) = client_ip {
951:         request = request
952:             .set_remoteip(&ip.to_string())
953:             .map_err(|e| ResponseError {
954:                 err: anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to build captcha request: {:?}", e),
955:                 response: Some((
956:                     StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
957:                     "Captcha validation failed.".into(),
958:                 )),
959:             })?;
960:     }
961: 
962:     let response = hcaptcha::Client::new()
963:         .verify(request)
964:         .await
965:         .map_err(|e| ResponseError {
966:             err: anyhow::anyhow!("Captcha verification failed: {:?}", e),
967:             response: Some((
968:                 StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,
969:                 "Captcha verification failed.".into(),
970:             )),
971:         })?;
972: 
973:     if response.success() {
974:         Ok(())
975:     } else {
976:         Err(ResponseError {
977:             err: anyhow::anyhow!("Captcha invalid: {:?}", response.error_codes()),
978:             response: Some((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Invalid captcha token.".into())),
979:         })
980:     }
981: }

content.text: I've explored enough to start. Issue #71's exact `EmailGateway` API is under-specified in ways that matter (the `build_message` signature, and how `alt_email_address` / `get_account_by_address` survives when only `account_by_type` is listed). Let me confirm the shape before coding.

questions.question: For the EmailGateway trait shape (#71), how should I resolve the under-specification?

options.label: 3 methods, builder-returning build_message, unified account lookup
options.description: Trait: send_messages(from, Vec<Message>); build_message(account) -> Result<MessageBuilder> (pre-set from+date, callers chain); account(&self, AccountLookup) where AccountLookup enum {ByType(EmailType), ByAddress(String)} — keeps alt_email_address working, stays 3 methods. MessageBuilder leaked into trait is OK.

options.label: Exactly as written, drop get_account_by_address
options.description: Trait: send_messages; build_message(account, ...) -> Result<Message>; account_by_type(EmailType). Remove alt_email_address-by-address support entirely — events with alt_email_address fall back to type lookup. Behavior change for events using alt_email_address.

options.label: 3 methods, account_by_type only, pass alt address via a lookup at call site
options.description: Trait: send_messages; build_message -> Result<MessageBuilder>; account_by_type(EmailType). get_associated_email_account stays on Event but uses RealEmailGateway's inherent account_by_address (concrete, not on trait) — tests using alt_email_address keep using real or a richer mock. Trait stays 3 methods.

questions.question: For the canonical mock constructor (#71), what should survive in test_utils.rs?

options.label: One flexible capturing mock
options.description: Keep one constructor: mock_email_gateway(accounts) returns (mock, Arc<Mutex<Vec<(EmailAccount, Vec<Message>)>>) — captures batches with account+messages. Tests that don't care about capture just ignore the returned Vec. Noop cases use MockEmailGateway::new() directly. Covers all current uses.

options.label: One mock, mode flag
options.description: mock_email_gateway(accounts, capture: CaptureMode) where CaptureMode is Noop|Discard|Capture|CaptureBatch — one constructor, explicit mode per test. More verbose at call sites but very explicit.

options.label: Keep discard + capturing as two
options.description: Two constructors survive: mock_email_gateway (discard) and mock_email_gateway_capturing (capture). Tests choose. Violates 'exactly 1 mock constructor' criterion but is most ergonomic.
